feature_name: v1.20.0 "Subscribe button -- real subscriptions from downloads": turns the cosmetic watch-page Subscribe button into a real toggle (with a compact options-confirm modal on subscribe) wired to the existing yt-dlp subscription system, driven by each file's captured creator/channel identity; surfaces each subscribed channel as its own Playlist via the existing physical per-channel download subfolder + root= filter; lowers DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS 3->2

wave_1.rationale: T1 (lib/ytdlp/{args,run,store,index}.js + server.js) and T5 (lib/ytdlp/config.js) are DISJOINT file sets -> run in parallel.

wave_2.rationale: After T1: T2 (public/js/common.js) and T4 (lib/ytdlp/index.js + lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js + public/css/style.css) are DISJOINT (T4's optional common.js sheet-link edit scoped OUT) -> run in parallel.

wave_3.rationale: After T2 AND T4: T3 touches public/js/common.js (conflicts T2), lib/ytdlp/index.js (conflicts T1->T4), public/css/style.css (conflicts T4) -> must run alone, last.

shared_tree_serialization.public/js/common.js: T2(W2) -> T3(W3); T4 does NOT touch it (scoped out)

git_ownership: Coordinator (EM) owns ALL git. SDE agents report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint results and do NOT commit/stage/branch.
base: main (v1.19.1). Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (most recently v1.19-ui-polish).
process_plan: Discovery completed by product-manager, grounded directly in the actual code (lib/ytdlp/*, server.js scan/synthetic-folder/embedded-tag logic, public/js/watch.js + common.js), not assumed from prior exec-plan wording. Dean's locked decisions (button-toggle-only trigger, hide-when-no-creator, per-channel Playlist, 2 as the new default N) are folded into the ACs as non-negotiable constraints. FR-1 was revised mid-Discovery per a relayed refinement from Dean: subscribing now opens a compact options-confirm modal (type/quality/maxVideos/skipShorts/filetype, channel identity read-only) rather than silently creating a subscription with hardcoded defaults -- see the exec plan's Decision log for the full note (including a flag that this refinement arrived via an unusual mid-task channel and should be confirmed genuine before it ships, since it revises what the original assignment had marked 'locked'). Two review tiers: TWO-REVIEWER GATE for FR-1 (new watch-page caller into the spawn-guarded subscription create/delete path), FR-2 (validating/parsing yt-dlp-supplied channel metadata before persistence -- an untrusted-input-hardening surface), and FR-4 (must re-prove the db.folders/folderSettings synthetic-root invariant holds); FR-3 rides FR-1's tier (same UI surface); FR-5 gets the LIGHTER single-QA tier (trivial constant change + regression lock, exact precedent: the v1.18.0 25->3 change).
hard_constraints: The channel URL that ever reaches a yt-dlp spawn -- whether user-typed (existing /subscriptions add form) or file-derived (new, FR-1/FR-2) -- MUST pass through store.validateSubscriptionInput -> url.validateChannelUrl unchanged. No new code path may construct a subscription record or spawn argv from an unvalidated string.

Channel metadata captured from yt-dlp's own output (FR-2, both the subscription-poll download path and the one-shot download path) is untrusted input the same way user input is: validated/bounded before persistence or use, never trusted blindly.

The db.folders-vs-folderSettings synthetic-root invariant (a prior data-loss-class bug) is unchanged: FR-4 must never write a per-channel Playlist entry into db.folders. Confirmed FR-4 is a PHYSICAL-subfolder mapping (yt-dlp already writes flat into a per-channel confined subfolder via args.resolveChannelDir) and GET /api/videos?root= already supports arbitrary subfolder path filtering (underFolder is a pure path-prefix match, not restricted to db.folders entries) -- no new backend filtering primitive is needed for FR-4.

Disabled-module byte-identical guarantee preserved: with FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off, no Subscribe button/modal, no Playlist entries, no new routes reachable -- native 404 on every backing route, exactly like every existing yt-dlp route.

Follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes, textContent over innerHTML for new dynamic strings (the modal's creator name/channel URL display especially), node:test for new/regression tests, lint 0 warnings, NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties) for the new modal; reuse the corrected v1.19.0 FR-1 select-sizing pattern (never reintroduce the oversized-mobile-select bug) and the v1.17.0 FR-6 full-teardown backdrop-dismiss pattern (never reintroduce a stuck-overlay bug) -- do not reimplement these from scratch.

FR-2's channel-identity matcher must be pure/unit-testable and must not rely on naive string equality of two channel URLs that may differ in shape (@handle vs /channel/UC... vs youtu.be) -- see the exec plan's Decision log for the recommended channel_id-based approach (PE's call on exact algorithm).

Files downloaded BEFORE this feature ships have no stored channelUrl -- documented, explicit limitation (best-effort embedded-tag fallback, else the Subscribe button is HIDDEN for that file, consistent with FR-3's hide rule). Reliable Subscribe-button behavior is for NEW downloads going forward only.

FR-5: DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS 3->2 for NEW subscriptions only -- 0=unlimited, the FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS env override, and already-persisted subscriptions' stored maxVideos are all unaffected (exact mirror of the v1.18.0 25->3 change and its regression tests).

product_forks_for_dean.fr1_modal_vs_direct_create: RESOLVED (revised mid-Discovery per a relayed refinement from Dean) -- subscribing opens a compact options-confirm modal (pre-filled type/quality='best'/maxVideos=2/skipShorts=off/filetype=default, channel identity read-only) rather than silently creating a subscription with hardcoded defaults; unsubscribing stays a direct one-tap DELETE (no modal). FLAGGED: this refinement arrived via an unusual mid-task channel (embedded mid-tool-call rather than as a normal new instruction) and revises what the original assignment had explicitly marked 'locked' -- benign/in-scope content (no security-guard change), but the EM/Dean should confirm it was genuinely intended before Design/Implementation proceeds.
product_forks_for_dean.fr2_oneshot_capture_scope: RESOLVED, not a genuine fork -- the feature's own stated goal explicitly calls out 'after a one-shot download you can subscribe to that creator,' so FR-2's channelUrl-capture work covers BOTH the subscription-poll download path (trivial -- sub.channelUrl is already known) AND the one-shot single-video download path (the larger sub-problem -- classifySingleVideo captures no channel metadata today). Recommended mechanism (PE's call): yt-dlp's --print flag emitting channel_url/channel_id/channel during the same download spawn (no extra network round-trip), captured via the SAME stdout line-splitting spawnYtdlpDownload already uses for progress parsing.
product_forks_for_dean.fr2_matching_key: RESOLVED (default), non-blocking -- recommend capturing/storing yt-dlp's stable channel_id alongside channelUrl/channelName and preferring channel_id-based matching when available, falling back to normalized-URL-shape comparison otherwise. Exact algorithm left to PE's Design section.
product_forks_for_dean.fr4_physical_vs_virtual: RESOLVED, not an open fork -- confirmed PHYSICAL per-channel subfolder by reading lib/ytdlp/args.js's resolveChannelDir + OUTPUT_TEMPLATE (flat, no nested path). GET /api/videos?root= already supports arbitrary subfolder filtering (underFolder is a pure path-prefix match, not restricted to db.folders entries) -- FR-4 needs zero new backend filtering primitive, only a per-subscription link surfaced client-side (likely via a new field on GET /api/subscriptions exposing the resolved channel dir -- left to PE).
product_forks_for_dean.fr1_unsubscribe_confirm: RESOLVED (default), non-blocking -- no extra inline confirm on unsubscribe; direct one-tap DELETE, matching Dean's original explicit FR-1 wording and low blast-radius (unsubscribing never deletes already-downloaded files, only stops future polling).

FR-1.title: Subscribe button = real toggle + compact options-confirm modal (watch page)
FR-1.review_weight: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial code-review) -- the watch page becomes a new caller into the spawn-guarded subscription create/delete system; must prove no path bypasses store.validateSubscriptionInput/url.validateChannelUrl.
files: public/js/common.js or a new shared module (modal option-control reuse from lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's buildFormatSelect/buildFiletypeSelect/reduceFiletypeOptions patterns)
public/css/style.css (modal styling, era tokens, corrected select-sizing)
test/unit + test/integration (button-state derivation, modal confirm->POST, cancel/backdrop teardown, unsubscribe->DELETE)

FR-2.title: Capture + use the file's creator/channel identity
FR-2.review_weight: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial code-review) -- validating/parsing yt-dlp-supplied channel metadata before persistence/use is an untrusted-input-hardening surface, not just a UI concern.
files: lib/ytdlp/args.js and/or run.js (capture mechanism -- e.g. --print on the download spawn)
lib/ytdlp/index.js (runSubscriptionCycle/runOneShot -- persist captured channelUrl/channelName/channelId per downloaded item)
server.js (scan: attach captured channel identity to db.metadata[id]; resolveChannelName precedence)
public/js/common.js (resolveChannelName precedence extension; pure channel-identity matcher)

FR-3.title: Hide the Subscribe button when there's no creator/channel
FR-3.review_weight: Rides FR-1's TWO-REVIEWER GATE tier (same UI surface, same button).
files: public/js/common.js (pure show/hide predicate, reusing FR-2's derivation)

FR-4.title: Each subscribed channel = a Playlist
FR-4.review_weight: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial code-review) -- must re-prove the db.folders/folderSettings synthetic-root invariant holds with the new per-channel Playlist links, even though nothing here loosens an existing guard.
files: lib/ytdlp/index.js and/or store.js (expose each subscription's resolved channel download dir, e.g. via GET /api/subscriptions)
public/js/common.js (Playlists-sheet / Subscriptions-tab per-channel link rendering, reusing the existing /?root=<path> pattern)
test/unit + test/integration (confinement -- one channel's playlist never surfaces another's videos; db.folders untouched)

FR-5.title: Default download count 3 -> 2
FR-5.review_weight: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression tier -- trivial constant change + regression lock, exact precedent: the v1.18.0 25->3 change.
files: test/unit (regression: 0=unlimited, env override, existing-subs-unaffected, mirroring the v1.18.0 25->3 tests)

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. All of FR-1/FR-2/FR-3/FR-4 need PE design (FR-3 rides FR-1's design work, sharing the show/hide predicate and FR-2's derivation). FR-5 does not need PE design (trivial constant change) but rides through Design for one cohesive plan. Key open engineering questions for PE: (1) FR-2's exact channelUrl/channelId capture mechanism for BOTH download paths (recommended: yt-dlp --print on the existing download spawn, parsed via the same stdout line-splitting spawnYtdlpDownload already uses for progress); (2) where captured channel identity is persisted between 'download completes' and 'scan indexes the resulting file' (the scan computes db.metadata ids independently of yt-dlp's own video id, so a bridge/lookup is needed -- the output filename already embeds yt-dlp's video id in brackets per OUTPUT_TEMPLATE, which cleanDisplayTitle already parses off for display, a candidate join key); (3) FR-1 modal's exact markup/DOM structure reusing lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's existing option-control builders without creating a circular dependency between public/js/ and lib/ytdlp/client/; (4) FR-4's exact mechanism for the client to learn a subscription's resolved channel download dir without duplicating sanitizeChannelName client-side (recommended: a new response field on GET /api/subscriptions).
cross_cutting: Disabled-module no-op preserved end-to-end (no Subscribe button/modal, no Playlist entries, no new routes reachable when FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED is off). Era-theme CSS vars for all new modal styling. No new npm dependencies; CommonJS server; vanilla DOM client code; node:test covers every new pure helper (channel matcher, channel-identity derivation, show/hide predicate, FR-5's constant). Lint 0 warnings. textContent (never innerHTML) for new dynamic strings. No headless-browser/E2E infra exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- FR-1's modal UX and FR-4's on-device Playlist confirmation lean on Dean's on-device pass as the documented arbiter, not a testing gap; everything else in this round is substantially code/test-verifiable (matcher/derivation/predicate unit tests; POST/DELETE route integration tests; the db.folders-exclusion regression suite).

tasks.title: FR-2 capture + store + bridge (ENABLER)
tasks.description: Capture each downloaded file's channel identity at yt-dlp download time via a fixed-literal --print after_move:FTCHMETA template on the download spawn (both audio/video, subscription + one-shot); parse it via the existing makeLineSplitter stream into a bounded channelMeta array; sanitize/validate every captured URL through the UNMODIFIED url.validateChannelUrl before persisting into db.ytdlp.downloadMeta (FIFO-capped); persist on both download paths in index.js (with sub.channelUrl/sub.name fallback on the subscription path); bridge onto db.metadata[id] at scan time via a new extractYtdlpVideoId helper + consumeDownloadChannelMeta (read-validate-delete).
tasks.gate: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (untrusted yt-dlp output validated before use; download-spawn argv change)

tasks.title: FR-2 client matcher + creator precedence
tasks.description: Pure client-side channel-identity primitives in public/js/common.js: canonicalizeChannelUrl (URL-shape -> canonical key), channelIdentityMatches (set-membership on canonical keys incl. channel:UCid), resolveFileChannelIdentity, and the resolveChannelName captured-channelName-first precedence extension (non-yt-dlp files unchanged). node:test unit coverage for each.
tasks.gate: Rides T1's FR-2 TWO-REVIEWER GATE tier (same security surface)

tasks.title: FR-1 subscribe toggle + compact options modal + FR-3 hide
tasks.description: public/js/common.js buildSubscribeModal (reusing the .oneoff-modal-* CSS + buildOneOffSelect/ONEOFF_*/reduceOneOffFiletypeOptions primitives already in common.js -- NO dependency on the gated /js/subscriptions.js) and shouldShowSubscribeButton; public/js/watch.js wiring (button state via channelIdentityMatches against GET /api/subscriptions, modal open/confirm->POST/full-teardown, one-tap unsubscribe->DELETE, FR-3 show/hide via the /api/subscriptions/health probe); public/watch.html default-hidden #subscribe-btn-mock; public/css/style.css read-only identity block (era tokens only); lib/ytdlp/index.js defaultMaxVideos on GET /api/subscriptions/health. Read-only channel identity via textContent; confirm routes through the UNMODIFIED validateSubscriptionInput/validateChannelUrl.
tasks.gate: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (watch page -> spawn-guarded subscription create/delete; no bypass of validateSubscriptionInput/validateChannelUrl)

tasks.title: FR-4 per-channel playlist
tasks.description: Enrich the gated GET /api/subscriptions handler (lib/ytdlp/index.js) with a computed channelDir = args.resolveChannelDir(config, sub) (try/catch; read-only; NEVER writes db.folders); render a /?root=<encodeURIComponent(channelDir)> playlist link per subscription row in lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js (anchor via textContent/escapeAttr); add a small distinct link-style block in public/css/style.css. The design's OPTIONAL mobile-Playlists-sheet link in public/js/common.js is scoped OUT to keep T4 disjoint from T2 on the shared tree (Subscriptions-tab link alone satisfies AC22).
tasks.gate: TWO-REVIEWER GATE (re-prove the db.folders/folderSettings synthetic-root invariant)

tasks.title: FR-5 default download count 3 -> 2
tasks.description: lib/ytdlp/config.js DEFAULT_MAX_VIDEOS 3 -> 2 and fix the stale v1.17.0 -> v1.18.0 precedent comment cite; new subscriptions only. Regression unit tests: 0=unlimited, FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS env override, already-persisted subs unaffected, new-subs-only (mirror the v1.18.0 25->3 tests).

history.note: ENGINEERING-MANAGER finalized the Tasks stage and advanced to Implementation. Read the principal-engineer's ## Design + proposed ## Task breakdown in docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-08-v1.20-subscribe.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md (testing strategy / no-E2E), and .state/feature-state.json. Confirmed the PE's 5-task decomposition (T1 FR-2 enabler / T2 FR-2 client matcher+precedence / T3 FR-1 toggle+modal+FR-3 hide / T4 FR-4 per-channel playlist / T5 FR-5 default 3->2) with one scoping refinement: T4's OPTIONAL mobile-Playlists-sheet edit to public/js/common.js is scoped OUT so T4 stays disjoint from T2 on the shared tree (Subscriptions-tab link alone satisfies AC22 per the design's own open-fork note). Recorded a 3-wave plan with full file-ownership serialization proving no two concurrently-running SDE agents ever edit the same file: Wave 1 (parallel) T1[args,run,store,index+server.js] + T5[config.js] DISJOINT; Wave 2 (parallel, after T1) T2[common.js] + T4[index.js,client/subscriptions.js,style.css] DISJOINT; Wave 3 (alone, after T2 AND T4) T3[common.js,watch.js,watch.html,style.css,index.js]. Serialization: lib/ytdlp/index.js T1->T4->T3; public/js/common.js T2->T3; public/css/style.css T4->T3. Dependency graph: T1->{T2,T4}; {T2,T4}->T3; T5 independent. Wrote the finalized ## Task breakdown (tasks + wave plan + serialization + dep graph) into the exec plan and the full tasks[] array + wave_plan + shared_tree_serialization into state. Wrote five task-suffixed, self-contained inbox files (.state/inbox/software-developer-T1.md .. T5.md), each noting: coordinator owns ALL git (SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 test/lint, do NOT commit); Node 22 toolchain bin at scratchpad/node-v22.23.1-linux-x64/bin; gate tier; scope/files/ACs/deps. Set stage=implementation, current_wave=1, current_tasks=[T1,T5]. Did NOT write application/test code and did NOT touch git. Wave 1 (T1+T5) ready to dispatch to two software-developer sessions in parallel.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery for v1.20.0 'Subscribe button -- real subscriptions from downloads' (feature/v1.20-subscribe, off main at v1.19.1). Read docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/RELIABILITY.md, the completed v1.11/v1.12 yt-dlp-module exec plans and v1.16-watch-experience for context, and grounded every AC directly in the live code (not assumed from prior wording): lib/ytdlp/{config,url,store,index,args,run,rules}.js in full; server.js's scan (scanDirRecursive/folderName/rootFolder derivation, cleanDisplayTitle's yt-dlp-download detection), EMBEDDED_TAG_WHITELIST/parseFfprobeTags, the synthetic-Downloads-folder GET/POST /api/config logic, and GET /api/videos's root=/folder= filters; public/watch.html's .uploader-info-panel/#subscribe-btn-mock (confirmed via grep it has ZERO click-handler wiring anywhere -- fully cosmetic today); public/js/watch.js's populateMetadata/resolveChannelName call site; public/js/common.js's resolveChannelName/rankRelated/Playlists-sheet code; lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js's existing add-subscription form option-control builders. Key grounded findings: (1) embedded ffprobe tags cannot reliably carry a channel URL (the 'comment' tag carries the video's own webpage_url, not a channel URL) -- confirms channelUrl must be captured at download time from yt-dlp's own metadata, not recovered after the fact; (2) yt-dlp downloads ALREADY land in a physical per-channel subfolder (args.resolveChannelDir, flat OUTPUT_TEMPLATE) -- resolves FR-4's stated physical-vs-virtual fork definitively in favor of physical; (3) GET /api/videos?root= already supports arbitrary subfolder path filtering (underFolder is a pure path-prefix match, not restricted to db.folders entries) -- FR-4 needs zero new backend filtering primitive; (4) subscription-driven downloads already know their channel URL trivially (sub.channelUrl in scope), but one-shot single-video downloads capture NO channel metadata today, and the feature's own stated goal explicitly requires one-shot downloads to be covered too -- flagged as the larger of FR-2's two capture sub-problems, with a recommended yt-dlp --print-based mechanism for PE. Confirmed no conflict with any other active exec plan, no contradiction with docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's documented key decisions (FR-4 is additive on top of the documented synthetic-scan-root design), and no open tech-debt-tracker item addressed/created. Authored docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-08-v1.20-subscribe.md: Goal/Scope/Out-of-scope/Constraints/32 grouped-and-tagged acceptance criteria across FR-1 (8) / FR-2 (6) / FR-3 (3) / FR-4 (5) / FR-5 (4) / Cross-cutting (6), plus a Decision log recording every grounding finding, the resolved (non-blocking) forks, and one flagged item: a mid-Discovery refinement to FR-1 (subscribe now opens a compact options-confirm modal rather than silently creating a subscription with hardcoded defaults) arrived via an unusual mid-task channel (embedded mid-tool-call rather than as a normal new instruction) -- its content is benign and in-scope (no security-guard change, consistent with the feature's stated goal) so it was incorporated into FR-1/ACs, but it revises what the original assignment had explicitly marked 'locked,' so it is flagged for the EM/Dean to confirm was genuinely intended before Design/Implementation proceeds. Flagged FR-1/FR-2/FR-4 for the two-reviewer gate (new watch-page caller into the subscription-create/delete system; untrusted yt-dlp-metadata parsing/validation; re-proving the db.folders/folderSettings invariant); FR-3 rides FR-1's tier; FR-5 recommended for the lighter single-QA tier (exact v1.18.0 25->3 precedent). Set artifacts.exec_plan/requirements/design to the new exec plan path; stage remains 'discovery' (did not self-advance). Did not write application/test code and did not touch git. Reporting back to EM for Design routing (/prep-pe-design).

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Acceptance for v1.20.0 'Subscribe button -- real subscriptions from downloads'. Read docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-08-v1.20-subscribe.md (32 ACs across FR-1..FR-5 + cross-cutting) and verified every criterion directly against the implementation: lib/ytdlp/{args,run,store,config,url,index}.js, server.js's scan bridge (extractYtdlpVideoId/consumeDownloadChannelMeta), public/js/{common,watch}.js, public/watch.html, public/css/style.css, lib/ytdlp/client/subscriptions.js, and the corresponding test/unit + test/integration suites (channel-identity.test.js, subscribe-button.test.js, ytdlp-run.test.js's FTCHMETA forgery regressions, ytdlp-channel-meta-bridge.test.js, ytdlp-channel-dir-enrichment/playlist.test.js, ytdlp-health-maxvideos-override.test.js, ytdlp-subscribe-watch-security.test.js, ytdlp-disabled-noop.test.js, ytdlp-config.test.js). Result: 32/32 ACs met (24 fully code/test-verified; 8 additionally carry an explicit, documented on-device confirmation for Dean per RELIABILITY.md's no-headless-browser-E2E posture -- none open/failing). Independently re-confirmed both prior adversarial findings are remediated: the HIGH capture-injection (CHANNEL_META_PRINT_TEMPLATE now emits a single JSON object via yt-dlp's .{...}j selector, parsing restricted to stdout-only, locked by dedicated forged-newline regression tests) and the MED env-default drift (GET /api/subscriptions/health's defaultMaxVideos now reflects config.maxVideos, the ENV-resolved effective default, not the bare constant -- locked by ytdlp-health-maxvideos-override.test.js's three cases). Confirmed security touchpoints hold: no new/parallel validator anywhere in the diff (every channel URL that can reach a spawn or persistence routes through the unmodified validateSubscriptionInput/validateChannelUrl), the db.folders/folderSettings synthetic-root invariant is untouched (enrichSubscriptionWithChannelDir is read-only/per-request, regression-locked), and every new dynamic string is textContent-only (static-source regression guard exists for buildSubscribeModal). Flagged the OLD-downloads limitation (no Subscribe button on pre-feature files, no stored channelUrl) as delivered-by-design per the exec plan's own Out-of-scope section and AC12, not a gap. Appended a full ## Acceptance section (per-FR AC-by-AC verdicts, security touchpoint re-confirmation, env-default re-confirmation, OLD-downloads call-out, overall verdict) to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-08-v1.20-subscribe.md. Verdict: ACCEPT-WITH-NOTES -- recommend advancing to Done; notes are for Dean's on-device pass (real yt-dlp --print output shape, modal feel, FR-4 playlist tap-through), none are blockers. Set stage to 'acceptance-complete'. Did not edit code, tests, or commit.
